subject: Re: 16-bit floating point tomograms in WarpTools
body:
Hi Jesse,
Environment Variables - Warp, M and friends
warpem.github.io
Cheers,
Alister
Sent from mobile - apologies for brevity
On Aug 8, 2024, at 16:03, Jesse Hansen <bluufive@gmail.com> wrote:
﻿hi all,
It seems warptools reconstructs tomos in 16-bit floating point, is that right?  I tried to do Dynamo template matching on this but Dynamo couldn't read the file.  It seems it can't do TM on this map mode?  I converted to 32-bit floating point using Imod but the Dynamo TM result was not as good as the windows WARP-generated tomos from the same dataset.
Is it possible to export 32-bit floating point tomos with Warptools?
thanks
Jesse
